rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MOITOZA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Cal-Steam, Inc. ¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence to create a triable issue that the packing and gaskets that plaintiff was exposed to that are attributable to defendant contained asbestos. The objections to paragraphs 18-19 and 21-22 of Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MIHALKO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant SBC Holdings, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings on the issue of the application of the Washington statute of limitations is denied. A motion for judgment on the pleadings is limited to matters that are apparent from the face of the complaint and matters that may be judicially noticed. The complaint alleges that ¿Plaintiff was diagnosed with asbestos-related pleural disease on or about 2008.¿ The matters sought be judicially noticed that contradict this allegation are not the proper subject of judicial notice. (Rutter Group Book on Civil Trial and Evidence 8:868-869). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WILSON et al VS. FOSTER WHEELER LLC (FKA FOSTER WHEELER CORPORATION)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s demurrer to second amended complaint is overruled. The apparent inconsistency with regard to the years of exposure attributable to defendant is resolved by plaintiffs¿ opposition memorandum which confirms that the applicable years are ¿between 1964 and 1970¿ and not ¿between 1962 and 1991.¿ The allegation of a postmortem diagnosis is not subject to challenge on a demurrer. = (220/HEK)
